children.text: Berlin-based dance night and sound system SHUSH present their first musical offering: six soulful tracks that encapsulate the unique spirit of their functions and underlying community. Atlanta house ambassador Stefan Ringer lends his touch on deep vocal cut ‘Focus’, electronic jazz maestro Ziggy Zeitgeist serves up the late-night coaster ‘Yours To Make’ (feat. Jitwam), whilst SHUSH’s own D’Monk deals the dubbed-out damage of ‘Love Or Lust’, a raw house cut heavy on the 909s. Alongside them, producer Whodat offers a slapper in her own distinctive Detroit-style, AMA//MIZU comes with an introspective downbeat roller whilst Dweller presents one of his stunning orchestral works.
Spread across two sides of a 12”, these bold selections translate their feeling both on and off the dancefloor.
B1	Ziggy Zeitgeist - Yours To Make (feat. Jitwam)
A2, A3, B3 & BONUS mixed by D'Monk
All other tracks mixed by the respective artist
Limited run of Risograph prints made for our 3 YEAR ANNIVERSARY Celebration in June.

It was a beautiful function, surrounded by our friends & family and including some seriously deep music. Thank you for everyone who's been supporting our movements over the years – we truly appreciate it.
Layout / Design by Anna Ntombi Marx & Ken Okuda.
Printed in Berlin on heavyweight paper (150 g/m²). A2 format (59,4 x 42 cm)
Pick-ups in Berlin & London can be arranged if you're local – please write us

It was a beautiful function, surrounded by our friends & family and including some seriously deep music. Thank you for everyone who's been supporting our movements over the years – we truly appreciate it.

events.title: SHUSH // Àbasé & Petter Eldh (live), Ken Okuda, Orlando Rosé

children.text: Our first function of the year – we're back with a dance at Bar Neun to kick off proceedings for 2026. Joining us are two close friends and some of the most adventurous jazz musicians we know in Berlin, presenting their artistry with a special club live set. Expect to be twisted up by
on bass and electronics with a rare performance.
Besides them, we’ll keep it in the family: SHUSH residents

soundtracking things in a soulful and deep way, venturing off into familiar and unfamiliar groove territory. It’ll be lights low and feet movin’ – come join us with an open mind and get lost in the dance.
The first of many gathering this year – we’re looking forward to kicking things off amongst our friends & family. Early bird tix (5€) live now. Don’t sleep on these.

image.alt: SHUSH // Abasé & Petter Eldh (live), Ken Okuda, Orlando Rosé

children.text:
As part of TB10: Touching Bass’ 10th Anniversary World Tour, Errol and Alex Rita's musical movement link with Berlin's SHUSH for a two-day weekender of live music, food and dance at 90Mil. 

The Berlin-based label, sound system and collective of music lovers & music makers first invited Errol & Alex Rita to spin at one of their dances in late 2022. What turned out to be a transformational night grew into a connection based upon both crew’s shared love of freedom of expression, togetherness, and soulful music.

Since 2022, SHUSH has been nurturing a community that approaches music and club nights without constraints to style, tempo, or form. In late 2024, they’ve presented their first musical offering ‘SHUSH Vol. 1’ – a collection of tracks that encapsulate the unique spirit of their functions and sounds.

For ten years, Touching Bass — the London-based club night (recently named one of the “best parties in the world” by FADER), NTS Radio staple, record label, concert series and curatorial studio — has centred patience, intentionality and community. TB10 extends this ethos globally, partnering with artists, venues and like-minded musical entities for a series of club nights, concerts and intimate gatherings.

The venue is near Jannowitzbrücke in Berlin (Mitte). The exact name and address will be shared with you separately. To enter the venue, you must be a member. If you're not already one, it will be possible to make a subscription at the door for a membership fee of 5€.

events.title: Joy Crookes Aftershow Party hosted by SHUSH

children.text: Berlin family! We’re back with one more soulful (midweek) boogie before we head into a holiday break. This time round we’re excited and honoured to be joining forces with the UK rising star
to host the aftershow party for her concert in Berlin on December 4th.

Joy is a dear friend and a wonderful artist in her own right. The Bangladeshi-Irish singer and producer smoothly melds a beautiful balance of Motown-esque soul in with the music she grew up with in London, a fusion of RnB, Folk, Hip-Hop & Dub: all the while, it is the unique and unmistakable tone of her voice that stands out front and centre. She’s currently on tour for her new album ‘Juniper’ which dropped this autumn. We’ll have her spin a rare DJ set for our function – something not to be missed.

– local hero and badass selector who’s been rocking parties in her native Berlin for the past 20 years. She’s been called one of Germany’s "female ambassadors of funk" for good reason – her vibrant style, technical finesse and seasoned taste has kept her gigging schedule busy and we’re happy to finally be able to host her.

will be steering the ship in the usual – ecletic and joyful – fashion. We’re setting shop at Bar Neun in the heart of Kreuzberg or the first time and can’t wait to have you all join us there. The venue has been a community favourite as of late and we’re excited to be hosting a night ourselves.
It’s been an eventful couple of months for us and we’re truly grateful for all the energy and presence we’ve been receiving and sharing. Come celebrate with us in SHUSH fashion to ring out the year.
Limited early bird tickets (4€) available now. See you in the dance.
––– DISCLAIMER: Tickets for the Joy Crookes concert at Tempodrom (4th December) and our Aftershow Party at Bar Neun must be purchased separately –––

image.alt: Joy Crookes Aftershow Party hosted by SHUSH

children.text: Following our first outing in London at Corsica Studios, we’re back in town for something special this December – hosting our second dance in the city, at The Bath House in Hackney.

Providing sonics will be none other than K15, someone we’ve been so excited to bring to SHUSH for a long time. Joining him is REO, an exciting passionate selector with a soulful sound at heart, and Sticky Dub, with a rare live-PA set, fusing his unique rhythms and lyrical expression. SHUSH co-founders Ken Okuda & D’Monk will round things out with a back-to-back session.
It’s going to be a special evening from 5pm ’til midnight, powered by Our Soul Soundsystem to make sure things are sounding tight.
We’re looking forward to seeing some familiar faces there.

events.title: SHUSH presents Kahil El’Zabar & Ethnic Heritage Ensemble

children.text: Presale is sold out but there will be tickets at the door!
Location details are shared on Instagram and Telegram
We’re thrilled to invite you to our first concert night in Berlin – featuring Chicago’s

When we say that hearing Kahil and his band live for the first time was life-changing experience for us, we mean it. As messengers of healing and as jazz musicians tracing the history of Black Music from blues to pop, avant-garde and beyond, their music carries a spiritual weight that is stunning and deeply uplifting at the same time. Having them play for us is an honour and we can't wait to witness their magic together with you.
Kahil El’Zabar is a master percussionist and drummer with a legacy like few others. He’s assembled his Ethnic Heritage Ensemble over 50 years ago. In its current iteration – featuring
on cello – it is a representation of Chicago’s thriving scene, deeply steeped in the city’s musical traditions whilst comfortable stretching out and carrying Kahil’s words and message.
Leading into the night we have one of Berlin’s most compelling composers and voices of contemporary jazz:
. She will present a set of material written for this night, alongside an ensemble featuring
. Her work blends jazz (BAM) with electronic, orchestral and multi-disciplinary works.
Join us on November 2nd for this special night of music.
IMPORTANT: To enter the venue, you must be a member. If you're not already one, it will be possible to make a suscription at the door for a membership fee of 5€.

We’re hosting this at a venue that many of you will be familiar with already – one of Berlin's most cherished community hubs, close to U+S Jannowitzbrücke. Information will be sent out to all ticket holders prior to the show.

image.alt: SHUSH presents KAHIL EL'ZABAR & ETHNIC HERITAGE ENSEMBLE

children.text: //// Presale is over but there will be 15€ tickets at the door ////

Good people! We’re thrilled to be inviting you to our next function – a real special one – taking place in Berlin on October 4th. FLOOR PLAY is an explorative celebration of community on the dancefloor, centered around joyful expression through sound & movement.

Stimulating us sonically are four bold voices: Antwerp-based DTM Funk, one of the most daring selectors we know, who’s been shaping the sonic landscape of his city (and beyond) for well over a decade; Theo from Cleveland, Ohio – one half of soulful house collective Smooth Talk, bringing that Midwest grit to Berlin; local talent Fanni Ferrari, known for eclectic taste and selection of records; and Ken Okuda, who's been coining SHUSH's sonic signature since the start through his immersive, wide-reaching sets.

Guiding us energetically is Berlin-based dance collective corps sonores: They’ll be offering a workshop early on in the evening to stir up loose energies, as well as offer glimpses into their own performative practice. Rooted in House culture and contemporary performance, their work explores rhythm, rupture, and resonance between bodies, sound, and space.

As usual, we have our own sound system carrying frequencies and vibrations at Studio dB. Food is available on site, and we invite folks of all ages to come join us. This function is part of TAG DER CLUBKULTUR festival, under its motto this year: “Bridging Realities – 35 Years of Movement”.
We can't wait to get together. See you on the floor.

We’re back with a lil’ late-night dance to soothe the cravings. For this one we’re keeping it simple: Soulful sounds and plenty of deep wax spun by two selectors at one of our favourite spots in the city.
Joining us is none other than
– local hero and badass selector who’s been rocking parties in her native Berlin for the past 20 years. She’s been called one of Germany’s
for good reason – her vibrant style, technical finesse and seasoned taste has kept her gigging schedule busy and we’re happy to finally be able to host her.

will be steering the ship in the usual – ecletic and joyful – fashion. For this dance, we’re back at 60Hz in Kreuzberg where we’ve already hosted two functions last year. It’s a beautiful spot with a wooden dance floor and a tight system to elevate sonics and keep bodies moving.
Also – we’re keeping prices low for this function. Limited 3€ tickets available now. Yes, 3€. No excuses.
We’re thrilled to announce our London debut with a true icon of UK music and one of our biggest musical inspirations: Dego.

As many of you know, the music born out of the UK capital has always been at the core of what we do. Especially those sounds out of West London in the late 90s — IG Culture, Kaidi Tatham, 4hero & co. – have been education and foundation for our nights, label, and sound system.

It’s a full circle moment for us to invite one of the architects of this movement – Dego – to spin at our first London night. He was at the forefront of the development of Jungle, Breakbeat and most notably, Bruk. In his music, he continues to fuse the live feel of artists such as Herbie Hancock with the harmonic sensibilities of Roy Ayers, the rawness of hip-hop and a heavy nod to the house music coming out of the US. His label, 2000black, is the epitome of his world — a diverse catalog rooted in rhythm and funk.

Alongside Dego, we’re excited to have our sister Saige Sounds – one our favourite soulful DJs in the city – play. For a special b2b situation, she’s joined by Ken Okuda. D’Monk will be playing one of his live sets – straight from the MPC and a mixing board – showcasing his trademark machine funk. To round things off, our own Orlando Rosé will be handling vibrations on wax.
Plus, carnival on Sunday? Can’t go wrong.
PRESALE IS OVER, THERE WILL BE TICKETS AT THE DOOR!

In June 2022 we dragged a sound system – one we had just finished setting up – into a beautiful loft in North Berlin and held our first function. We didn’t know what we were up to at that moment, but we had conviction and belief. Alexander Nut – our first ever guest – did just what we had imagined: Soundtrack a dance that is meant to be soulful, with no constraints to style, tempo or form.

Witnessing the beauty of that gathering, we channelled its DIY spirit and kept on moving. Inviting many of our favourite selectors and musicians to join us on stage or behind the decks, we felt like our mission grew larger through the many dances we held. A community of likeminded people was forming around us, and in November 2024 our compilation SHUSH Vol. 1 found its way into the world, etching our musical ethos into wax.

On June 14, we’re inviting you to very special gathering at Studio dB in Berlin – to celebrate our third anniversary. To mark the occasion, we’ve put together a musical programme that we couldn’t be more thrilled about.

Making his SHUSH return is one of the most exciting and uncompromisingly imaginative selectors and producers, Julion De’Angelo from Detroit. Through a string of releases (on his own label Maybee Hill Records, as well as imprints such as Sound Signature or Vision Recordings) and his fabled sets, he’s been commanding dancefloors worldwide.

Next to him, we’re honoured to present the live debut of Oraculu – led by dear friend and talented producer/DJ Two Thou. With a spellbinding release last year, the experimental outfit sits in the space between jazz and electronics. Featuring likeminded souls Monica Mussungo and El Congo Allen, this performance will be one for the books.

If you’ve been to our dances, SHUSH co-founders and resident DJs Ken Okuda, Orlando Rosé and D’Monk are no strangers to you and your eardrums, all bringing their distinct flavours and sensibilities to the table. Last but not least, we have two of Berlin’s most revered percussionists – Ekowmania & K’Boko – joining by injecting their rhythm and feel to the dancefloor.

To celebrate the occasion, we will have limited merch – screen-printed posters and shirts – for sale. Deepest gratitudes to the immensely talented Nick Dahlen wo worked with us to craft this beautiful artwork, and to Anna Ntombi Marx for the poster design.
That’s it for now. We will be keeping you updated, and we’re looking forward to celebrating with you on June 14.
Next Friday! We’re gassed to present our own late-night takeover during this year’s XJAZZ! Festival, focusing on soulful sounds from across the spectrum over two floors.
Joining us is none other than one of dance music’s most prolific artists, Detroit house royalty Kyle Hall, off the back of a string of impeccable releases.

Next to him, Berlin-based electronic musician and DJ, as well as Tresor resident The Neighbourhood Character is featured, alongside tastemaking selector Bulma Brief from Milan. Completing the line-up are our own Ken Okuda  & D’Monk with a b2b set, bringing the distinct flavour of our nights to XJAZZ!.

We recommend you check out the whole festival line-up this year, featuring (amongst many other acts) Brown Penny, Moor Mother & Lonnie Holley, corto.alto, Alabaster DePlume, Ex Generation: Napoli Exchange or a first-ever Musclecars live show.
Full festival pass available at https://www.xjazz.net/events.
On the night, there’ll also be tickets on the door at just 10€.
Presale is over but there will be tickets at the door!

Join us on 5 April we’ll be taking our sound system back out to host you at our beloved stomping grounds, Studio dB. This time round we’re honoured to feature multi-faceted artist & extensive selector Baba T behind the decks, as well as join forces with two figures of the Berlin House Dance community for a special workshop & session.

As the key figure behind London-based radio show and dance night Don’t Touch My Fro!, Babajide Tikare aka ‘Baba T’ has fostered a platform that celebrates Black diasporic music in all its forms. The carefully curated line-ups (featuring many of our favourite artists) he’s been co-crafting and the attention to detail speak for themselves – we feel that this will be a truly inspirational link up.

Joining him are SHUSH residents Ken Okuda and Orlando Rosé, stretching out in a soulful way as usual, bringing with them a healthy stash of new discoveries. Lastly, we’re excited to have Berlins House Dance community on board for this function. Dancer & educator Alessia d’Isanto will be offering an introductory class, with dancer & DJ Achraf Lansari providing the steppers’ soundtrack early on.

As we’re trying to keep entry as accessible as possible, we’re offering two tickets: A discounted one (10€) , as well as a ‘supporter‘ ticket at a slight extra cost (15€) to help us throw these functions sustainably. Extra income will go to the artists directly – we appreciate your support if you can afford it.
after an exhilarating end to last year – including the release of our very first sonic artifact, the compilation 'SHUSH Vol. 1‘ – we’re back to keep it moving with our first dance of 2025.

Gathering at the beloved stomping grounds of Studio dB again, we’re dusting off our sound system to welcome you to another day-into-night affair. Our guests this time are not so much guests as they’ve been part of our fam for a long time – and we’re thrilled to be welcoming them in the booth on February 1st.

Originally from Los Angeles and arriving in Berlin via Brussels, Paulita has been showcasing her soulful style at various spots in the city, and through her sets on Kiosk Radio. Two years ago we had her join us for our stage at Gaswerk Art Days festival, where she spun us out when she dropped the freaky underground club anthem „Micro Wave Boogie“ early on, setting the tone for the night.

Playing alongside her is Viktor Roshu, who served up a very personal & uplifting selection at our Refuge Worldwide radio takeover last year, which was one of our highlights. We’re looking forward to seeing these two selectors share their musical bond and joy through the records that will be played.

Our own Ken Okuda will take care of the rest of the musical agenda – stretching out in the usual fashion while keeping the groove rolling. Doors open at 4pm, alongside musical nourishment we’ll have food to serve, plus there’ll be a limited supply of records and merch to grab (come early!).

We can't wait – see you in the dance
x

*** As we’re trying to keep the dance as accessible as possible, we’re offering two pre-sale ticketst this time: A discounted one, as well as a 'supporter‘ ticket which has a slight extra cost to help us throw these functions sustainably. Extra income will go to the artists directly – we'd appreciate your support if you can afford it. ***
Presale is over but there will be tickets at the door. Don't forget that the event is an early one, from 4pm to 1am.

Yesss, we’re absolutely thrilled to announce that we are releasing our first record in November and to celebrate, we’re throwing a day-into-night get down with the one and only Shy One from London as our special guest on the 16th of November.

Shy One’s sound perfectly embodies the diverse, raw & soulful energy that’s been at the core of our nights in the last couple of years and it’s an absolute honour to have her spin for such a special day for us. It’s full circle, as when we first started doing this, she was definitely at the top of our list. Two of our SHUSH crew members, Annalisa & D’Monk, even connected for the first time at one of her sets, being the only ones in the room at Panorama Bar whaling away the lyrics when she played 'Stuck' by Peven Everett. Music is a connecting force!

Our first time at Studio dB, we can’t wait get together in one of Berlin’s most cherished spaces and an all-around beautiful location. There’ll be food available on site, plus for this one it’s definitely time to be  bringing our trusted sound system back out.

events.title: SHUSH x Sonar Kollektiv present: HEATWAVE
